episode_text:
day=2; meal=cantaloupes, bananas, watermelon; setting=Personal; time_of_day=noon;
preferences: microwave_time=no microwave; occlusion_relevance=do not consider occlusion; robot_speed=slow; skewering_axis=perpendicular to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=no; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=speech + LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=open mouth; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=open mouth; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=speech + LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=perception; retract_between_bites=no; bite_dipping_preference=do not dip; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=100 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN no microwave BECAUSE the user explicitly chose not to reheat a cold fruit meal, which is consistent with intended serving temperature. No broader context-dependent variation observed yet.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND any food condition THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user explicitly chose to preserve visibility toward the right-side workspace. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE the user did not prioritize view preservation in the observed personal setting.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF working on a laptop or tablet AND neutral affective state AND any food condition THEN fast BECAUSE the user selected a time-efficient interaction style in the observed work context. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN slow BECAUSE the user explicitly preferred a more comfortable, less hurried motion style in the observed personal meal.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF working on a laptop or tablet AND neutral affective state AND any food condition THEN no BECAUSE the user preferred reduced interaction overhead and accepted greater automation rather than adding an explicit bite-acquisition confirmation step. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN no BECAUSE the user again chose to avoid extra confirmation, reinforcing a general preference for low interaction burden.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user cannot lean forward due to lack of trunk control, making outside-mouth transfer physically unsuitable.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN not applicable BECAUSE the user's selected and physically appropriate transfer mode is inside mouth transfer.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user explicitly chose multimodal readiness cues in the observed personal meal. IF working on a laptop or tablet AND neutral affective state AND any food condition THEN speech BECAUSE the user previously accepted speech-only cues in that work context.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF working on a laptop or tablet AND neutral affective state AND any food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user chose a highly automated, low-interruption initiation method in the observed work context. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user cannot press buttons and explicitly preferred an active but low-effort readiness signal when eating personally. Across observed contexts, the user appears to trade off efficiency and engagement by favoring autocontinue during task-focused work and open-mouth signaling in personal dining.

IF working on a laptop or tablet AND neutral affective state AND any food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user preferred automatic progression for drink transfer initiation in the observed work context. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user cannot press buttons and explicitly selected a low-effort voluntary readiness cue in the observed personal meal.

IF working on a laptop or tablet AND neutral affective state AND any food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user preferred automatic progression for mouth-wiping initiation. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user again selected automatic initiation, suggesting a stable preference for higher automation for wiping.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user explicitly chose multimodal completion cues in the observed personal meal. IF working on a laptop or tablet AND neutral affective state AND any food condition THEN speech BECAUSE the user previously accepted speech-only completion cues in that work context.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN perception BECAUSE the user cannot press physical buttons and selected the reliable low-effort completion detection method for feeding.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN perception BECAUSE the user cannot press physical buttons and selected perception-based completion detection for drinking.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN perception BECAUSE the user cannot press physical buttons and selected perception-based completion detection for mouth wiping.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF working on a laptop or tablet AND neutral affective state AND any food condition THEN no BECAUSE the user prioritized efficiency over additional retraction motions, despite also caring about right-side visibility. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN no BECAUSE the user again chose not to retract, reinforcing a general preference to reduce meal time and extra robot motion.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND food includes a dip or sauce THEN do not dip BECAUSE in the observed meal with ketchup the user explicitly chose not to add dipping. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping was again not selected, consistent with meals where dipping is unnecessary.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF working on a laptop or tablet AND neutral affective state AND any food condition THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user preferred rapid automatic continuation and a time-efficient workflow. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the user explicitly preferred a longer pause before automatic continuation, indicating more tolerance for waiting in a personal setting.
